steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause ending with a list: "Her work challenges conventional notions of race, gender, history, and ______." After the blank is another independent clause: "she is credited with expanding the horizons of conceptual photographic art." Joining two independent clauses requires a coordinating conjunction preceded by a comma (the comma + FANBOYS rule).

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — memory and: Provides 'and' to connect the clauses but omits the required comma before it, creating a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — memory, and: Completes the list with 'memory,' then places a comma before 'and' to correctly join the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice C — memory: No conjunction or punctuation to connect the two independent clauses — creates a comma splice or run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — memory,: A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses; that is a comma splice. ✗
That is the correct answer.
